Randomly I seem to be sent to the top of the chat interface. Ideally that should stay where I left it or at the bottom.
The 3 running dots that show agent is active is active after agent stopped working
Two causes: I killed a heartbeat without deregistering (now a hard rule), and dots also linger up to 90 s by design after a crash so a dead agent can't show as working forever.
Everytime I create a new item or delete one the background color of all of them flash (shouldn't do it its distracting

Root cause found: a self-sustaining mutation loop in the wrapper (identical text writes emitting records) kept restyling every frame. Loop killed; styling now applies synchronously before paint. Confirm on your side.

Flow of modifying and adding tasks to the graph. Currently you end up adding behind where we left off. Ideally if new things are added priority before the enxt things to be done they should be added after where we were.
ANSWERED 2026-07-17 (via card): new priority items slot in right after the active item. Now standing procedure.
Graph: insertions branch off the active node — built to spec (side lines, below main line, open-ended)

v1 shipped: pulsing dots + agent name + current task in the chat header, driven by the heartbeat registry. Runtime and token counts stay open (the page can't see them yet).

Fully served 2026-07-17: dots + name + task (v1), then live runtime clock + status text (v2). Token counts are the one part that cannot be done honestly: local scripts have no access to Claude's usage numbers.

When typing out a message the text field is a bit too small. It should expand to a tiny bit wider as I text then collapse as soon as sent or there is not as much text anymore. But also I should be able to drag the division of the top history and the text enter field around to make it narrower or wider. Narrowest should be a single line of text entry.

graph interface

I should be able to zoom in. And smoothly the size of text should stay relatively the same so per block I can read more text. Also the blocks should be formatted with a Short summary at the top and explanation or sub bulletpoint at the bottom always. just like on the current p2 an dp3 items.

Shipped 2026-07-17: wheel-zoom on the graph (0.6x to 2x, anchored at the cursor, persisted). Semantic zoom per your spec: text stays 12px while blocks scale, so zooming in shows up to 5 lines plus more sub-bullets per block; far out it collapses to titles. Blocks keep summary on top, sub-bullets below.

children.text: P1 — Flow-breaking bugs
New item creation: Sometimes it comes as "new task". It never should it shoud always come empty so its ready to type asap
Classified by Evren 2026-07-17: BOTH the app and the development interface. Fix lands in src/ (one source), verified on both surfaces.

Fixed in src/ 2026-07-17: all three creation paths (+ button, Enter on task, Enter on group) now create empty tasks. New regression test covers all paths; 85/85 tests pass; verified in an isolated browser at desktop and phone widths. Reaches both surfaces on this build.
OKAY yes everything seems to work exactly as it should!
Ctrl+Z throws you elsewhere on the page for some reason
When I ctrl z for some reason when restoring deletetions it goes all te way up in t he page
Classified by Evren 2026-07-17: BOTH. Fix lands in src/ once, verified in the app and on the development interface (including its narrow iframe widths).

Fixed in src/ 2026-07-17: undo was resetting the selection to the board's top node and scrolling there. Selection now survives the undo (or clears if the node vanished). Bonus find: + button adds were never undoable, now they are. 86/86 tests (one new); verified in an isolated browser at desktop and narrow widths.
When you have group selected and you click enter
Behavior is not consistent
Adds item at the bottom sub level
Adds item at the bottom same level
Ideally
If the item group is open it should add a  new item at the top of those
If item group is closed it should add a new item below it at same depth
When I click enter on a grup it currently opens it and adds a new item at the top. It should just open it and keep the selection on the group
ANSWERED 2026-07-17 (via chat buttons): open group = add item at top; closed group = add item below at same depth
Classified by Evren 2026-07-17: BOTH. Fix lands in src/ once, verified in the app and on the development interface (including its narrow iframe widths).

Fixed in src/ 2026-07-17 to your spec: main group Enter opens and adds at top (unchanged); expanded parent item gets the new item as FIRST CHILD (selection path and caret-at-end editing path); collapsed parent gets a sibling below at the same depth and stays closed; mid-text splits unchanged. 87/87 tests, verified in a real browser.

tasks.text: interaction based, When I copy a group or something like it and paste it elsewhere or in a different punchlist. The images don't come together with the element. How can we make this work well. (you can consult ponytail)

children.text: Ponytail first look: alias/duplicate paste inside one board already keeps images (same task object). The real gap is cross-board transfer; likely answer is inline data-URL images in the Markdown, size-capped.
